title: Breitmann About Town
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Schwackenhammer coom to down,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pefore de Fall vas past,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und by der Breitmann drawed he in
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ash dreimals honored gast.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Ledן¿½s see de sighdts! In self und worldt,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½s ן¿½sighdtsן¿½ for him, to see,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who Selbstanschauungsvermן¿½gen hat,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented to de Opera Haus,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dere dey vound em blayinן¿½,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of Offenbach (der open brook),
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His show spiel Belle Helן¿½ne.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dereן¿½s Offenbach, Sebastian Bach,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit Kaulbach, dat makes dree:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I alvays like sooch brooks ash dese;ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented to de Bibliothek,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhich Mishder Astor bilt:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Some pooks vere only en broschure,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und some vere pound und gilt.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dat makes de gold dat makes de sinn,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit pooks, ash men, ve see,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De pest tressed vellers guilt de most:ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent to see an edidor,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Whoן¿½d shanged his flag und doon,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und crowed oopon der oder side,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat very afdernoon.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½De anciends vorshipped wettercocks,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To wetter fanes pent de knee;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pow down, mein Schwackenhammer, pow!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented by a pankerן¿½s hause,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und Schwackenhammer shvore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He only vant a pig red shield
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hoong oop pefore de toor;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ One side of red, one side of gold,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like de knighds in hisdorie
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½De schildern of dat schild is rich,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent oonto a bicture sale.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of frames wortן¿½ many a cent,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De broperty of a shendleman,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who oonto Europe vent.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Donן¿½t gry heן¿½ll soon pe pack again
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit anoder gallerie:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He sells dem oud dwelf dimes a year,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented to dis bersonן¿½s house,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To see his furnidure,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Sold oud at aucdion rite afay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Beremdory und sure.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½He geeps six houses all at vonce,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Each veek a sale dere pe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gotts! vot a dime his vife moost hafe!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent to vind a goot cigar,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Long dimes dey roamed apout,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Von veller had a pran new sort,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De fery latest out
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Mein freund I dinks you errs yourself
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De shmell ish oldt to me;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat Infamias Stinkadores brand,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented to de virst hotel,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De prandy make dem creep,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A trop of idן¿½s enough to make
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A brazen monkey veep.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dey say a viner house ash dis,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vill soon ge-bildet pe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Crate Gott! vot can dey mean to trink?ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented droo de Irish shtreeds,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey saw vrom haus to haus,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und gountet oop, ן¿½pout more or less,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vive hoondred awful rows.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½If all dese liddle vights dey waste,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Could von crate pattle pe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gotts! how de Fenian funds vouldt rise!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitmann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent to see de Ridualisds,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who vorship Gott mit vlowers,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In hobes heן¿½ll lofe dem pack again,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In winter among de showers.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Vhen de Pacific railroatן¿½s done,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dis dings imbrofed vill pe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De joss-sticks vill pe santal vood,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent to hear a breecher of
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De last sensadion shtyle,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Twas ן¿½nough to make der teufel weep
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To see his ן¿½awful shmile.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Vot bities dat der Fechter neן¿½er
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas in Thן¿½ologie,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Deyן¿½d make him pishop in his shoorsh,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent indo a shpordinן¿½ crib,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De rowdies cloostered dick,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey ashk him dell dem vot oן¿½glock,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dat infernal quick.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Breitmann drawן¿½d his ן¿½volver oud,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ash gool ash gool couldt pe,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Idן¿½s shoost a goinן¿½ to shdrike six,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent polidן¿½gal meedins next
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey hear dem rant and rail,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der bresident vas a forger,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shoost bardoned oud of jail.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He does it oud of cratitood,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To dem who set him vree:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Idן¿½s Harmonie of Inderesds,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vent to a clairfoyand witch,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A plack-eyed handsome maid,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She wahrsagt all deir vortunes denn
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Fife dollars, gents!ן¿½ she said.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dese vitches are nod of dis eartן¿½,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und yed are on id, I see,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Shakesbeare knew de preed right vell,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented to a restaurand,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der vaiter coot a dash;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He garfed a shicken in a vink,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und serfed id at a vlash.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dat shap knows vell shoost how to coot
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und roon mit poulderie,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He vas copitain oonder Turchin vonce,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented to de Vomanן¿½s Righds,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere laties all agrees,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De gals should all pe voters,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und deir beaux all de votees.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½For efery man dat nefer vorks,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Von frau should vranchised pe:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat ish de vay I solfe dis ding,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey vented oop, dey vented down,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tvas like a roarinן¿½ rifer,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De sighds vere here de sighds vere dere-
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de vorldt vent on forefer.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½De more ve trinks, de more ve sees,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dis vorldt a derwisch pe;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Das Werdenן¿½s all von whirling droonk,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Said Breitemann, said he.
meaning:
About: Charles Godfrey Leland was an American humorist and folklorist, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. He was educated at Princeton University and in Europe.

Leland worked in journalism, travelled extensively, and became interested in folklore and folk linguistics, publishing books and articles on American and European languages and folk traditions. Leland worked in a wide variety of trades, achieved recognition as the author of the comic Hans Breitmannן¿½s Ballads, fought in two conflicts, and wrote what was to become a primary source text for Neopaganism half a century later, Aradia, or the Gospel of the Witches.


Source:- Wikipedia.
